audit_log.check: Autonomy vs Automation: PASS (formula selection context-based, not random)
Consciousness Awareness: PASS (formulas mapped to Neuro-Box 6D activation)
Quality Standards: PASS (no clickbait, honest promises, preview synergy)
No Manipulation: PASS (comprehensive clickbait detection, emotional manipulation flagged)
A/B Testing Integrity: PASS (statistical significance, ethical comparison)

dependency.integration_points: 6 base subject formulas expanded to 12, builds on detect_clickbait tool
dependency.notes: Block 5 is deep-dive extension of Block 3's subject line framework

consciousness_profile.description: Subject lines are first point of contact; different formulas activate different Neuro-Box dimensions to engage subscribers at various consciousness levels

formula_group.formulas: Curiosity Gap, Pattern Interrupt, Curiosity + Benefit
formula_group.consciousness_effect: Activates attention without triggering defense; safe to explore
Engages both emotion (story/experience) and intellect (question to answer)
Appeals to rational decision-making and desire for meaningful outcomes

formula_group.dimensions: Superior (better way exists) + Supported (others know this)
formula_group.consciousness_effect: Challenges status quo, offers superior alternative, validates with authority/community

consciousness_profile.convection_notes: Subject lines drive forwards/shares ("Did you see this subject?"); curiosity-based subjects get shared; personal/story subjects get discussed

purpose.primary: Expand Block 3's 6 subject formulas to 12 formulas with detailed usage guidance
purpose.secondary: Implement A/B testing protocol for subject line optimization
purpose.tertiary: Enhance clickbait detection beyond Block 3 (emotional manipulation, authority claims, guilt triggers)

when_to_use.scenario: Subject line optimization (improve open rates systematically)
Training team on subject line best practices

not_for.item: Complete email structure (use KB-EMAIL-ARCHITECTURE for full email)

principle.statement: Subject line creates expectation that MUST be met in opening 1-3 sentences. Violating this = trust destruction.
principle.rationale: Constitutional compliance (consent_intent); clickbait damages long-term open rates; delivered promises = subscriber respect

principle.statement: Subject should be specific enough to repel non-relevant readers. Filtering = healthy list; tricking = weak engagement.
principle.rationale: Specific subjects attract right people, repel wrong people = higher quality engagement; vague subjects = high opens, low clicks

principle.n: Test to Learn, Not to Trick
principle.statement: A/B testing finds what resonates, not what manipulates. Statistical significance required; ethical comparison mandatory.
principle.rationale: Testing without significance = noise; clickbait winning tests = short-term gain, long-term loss; learn preferences, don't exploit

principle.statement: Subject + preview text should complement (provide additional context), not repeat. Combined <100 chars ideal.
principle.rationale: Preview text is "subject line part 2" on mobile; repetition wastes space; synergy = more information = better decision to open

requirements.req: Each formula has pattern, examples, when_to_use, neuro_activation
Formulas cover all Neuro-Box 6D dimension pairs

standard.one_variable_rule: Test 1 variable at a time (formula, length, tone, etc.)

guardrail.rule: All subject lines MUST pass detect_clickbait_comprehensive() with severity < "medium"
guardrail.includes: Block 3 patterns + emotional manipulation + authority claims + guilt triggers

guardrail.rule: A/B test winners MUST achieve >10% lift AND >95% confidence before declaring
guardrail.why: Statistical significance prevents false positives; premature declarations = wasted learning

guardrail.rule: Subject + preview text combined MUST be <100 chars for mobile display

framework.purpose: Complete formula library covering all use cases and Neuro-Box activations

examples.ex: "The weird reason your emails go to spam"
"How to write emails people read (in 20 min/week)"
"How to launch without a huge list"
"Losing 10k subscribers taught me this about email"
"A 2am panic attack changed how I think about launches"

formula.when: Story emails, personal narrative, origin stories

examples.ex: "3 subject lines that tripled my opens"
"What sleeping in has to do with your open rates"
"The toaster analogy that changed my launches"

formula.why_works: Pattern interrupts brain expects one topic, gets two unrelated; forces attention; memorable if connection revealed

examples.ex: "The weird trick that doubled my engagement"
"The email flaw that actually boosts conversions"

formula.why_works: Combines curiosity (what's the surprising thing?) with benefit (outcome I want)

formula.when: Credibility building, lessons learned, experience sharing
formula.why_works: Personal authority + specific learning; implies valuable hard-won knowledge

examples.ex: "Why 'email daily' advice is killing your list"
"Why most subject line tips are backwards"

formula.why_works: Contrarian stance signals insider knowledge; challenges status quo; creates "us vs them" dynamic
formula.warning: MUST have good reason/evidence; contrarian for sake of it = clickbait

formula.when: Quick wins, time-based offers, urgency (if real)
formula.why_works: Specific timeframe = concrete promise; faster = more attractive; creates urgency
formula.warning: Timeframe MUST be realistic; overpromising = trust damage

formula.why_works: Clear value promise; "inside" creates anticipation; specific number/resource = tangible

formula_selection_matrix.use_case: Formulas 1, 2, 5, 9 (Curiosity Gap, Benefit-Driven, Numbered, Personal Experience)
Formulas 3, 7, 9 (Story Hook, Pattern Interrupt, Personal Experience)

framework.purpose: Systematic subject line optimization via controlled testing

testing_methodology.principle: Test ONE variable at a time (formula, length, tone, specificity)
testing_methodology.min_sample: 1,000+ subscribers per test (500+ per variant minimum)
testing_methodology.duration: 24 hours OR 500+ sends per variant (whichever comes first)

pair.tests: Which works better for your audience: intrigue or clear benefit?

example.a: "The weird reason your emails go to spam"
example.b: "How to land in inbox (not spam folder)"

pair.tests: Does asking engage better than telling?

example.b: "How to fix your email strategy this week" (42 chars)

pair.tests: Does personal authority beat universal application?

iteration_process.step: 1. Select test pair based on current knowledge gaps
3. Split list randomly (50/50 or 80/20 for cautious tests)
4. Send simultaneously (same time, remove timing variable)
5. Wait 24 hours or 500+ opens per variant
7. If winner (>10% lift, >95% confidence): Adopt winner, test next variable
8. If no clear winner: Retest or move to different variable

statistical_validation.formula:
# Lift calculation lift = ((variant_b_open_rate - variant_a_open_rate) / variant_a_open_rate) * 100
# Confidence interval (simplified z-test for proportions) # Use online calculator or statistical library for precise p-value # General rule: >10% lift + sample size >500 per variant = likely significant
statistical_validation.tools: Use statistical calculator for p-value (two-proportion z-test)

framework.purpose: Extend Block 3's detect_clickbait with additional manipulation patterns

category.why_clickbait: Exploits emotions for clicks vs delivers value; manipulative
False authority claims; unverified expert attribution; conspiratorial tone
Shame/guilt manipulation vs helpful guidance; attacks subscriber

examples.ex: "The one thing you need to know"

category.why_clickbait: Overused, vague, grandiose claims; lacks specificity

severity_scoring.medium: Score 3-5 - Multiple patterns OR severe single pattern
severity_scoring.high: Score 6-8 - Heavy clickbait, multiple severe patterns

orchestration_steps.step: 1. Select formula based on email purpose (educational, story, promo, etc.)
2. Generate 3-5 subject variants using selected formula
6. Create preview text that complements subject (validate_preview_synergy)
7. Select top 2 variants for A/B testing

tool.purpose: Generate multiple subject line variants for A/B testing
tool.validation_logic:
def generate_subject_variants(base_subject, variant_count=3, variation_type='formula'): """ Generates subject line variants for testing.
Variation types: - formula: Apply different formulas to same concept - length: Short, medium, long versions - tone: Personal vs universal, question vs statement
Returns: dict: { 'variants': list, 'formula_used': list, 'variation_type': str } """ import re
variants = [] formulas = []
# Extract key concept from base subject # Simple extraction: remove common words, keep content words words = base_subject.lower().split() stopwords = ['the', 'a', 'an', 'to', 'and', 'or', 'but', 'in', 'on', 'at', 'how', 'why'] key_words = [w for w in words if w not in stopwords] concept = ' '.join(key_words[:3]) # Take first 3 key words as concept
if variation_type == 'formula': # Generate variants using different formulas # Formula 1: Curiosity Gap variants.append(f"The surprising truth about {concept}") formulas.append('curiosity_gap')
# Formula 2: Benefit-Driven variants.append(f"How to {concept} (without the usual headaches)") formulas.append('benefit_driven')
# Formula 4: Question variants.append(f"Ready to master {concept}?") formulas.append('question')
# Formula 5: Numbered variants.append(f"3 ways to improve your {concept}") formulas.append('numbered')
# Formula 9: Personal Experience variants.append(f"What I learned from {concept}") formulas.append('personal_experience')
elif variation_type == 'length': # Short version (<40 chars) short = ' '.join(key_words[:2]) variants.append(f"{short.title()}") formulas.append('short')
# Medium version (40-60 chars) variants.append(base_subject[:60]) formulas.append('medium')
# Long version (detail added, still <60) variants.append(f"{base_subject[:50]} (+ tips)") formulas.append('long')
elif variation_type == 'tone': # Personal tone (I, my) variants.append(f"My {concept} breakthrough") formulas.append('personal')
# Universal tone (your, the) variants.append(f"Your {concept} guide") formulas.append('universal')
# Question tone variants.append(f"Struggling with {concept}?") formulas.append('question')
# Trim to requested count variants = variants[:variant_count] formulas = formulas[:variant_count]
return { 'variants': variants, 'formula_used': formulas, 'variation_type': variation_type }

tool.purpose: Extended clickbait detection including emotional manipulation, authority claims, guilt triggers
tool.validation_logic:
import re
def detect_clickbait_comprehensive(subject_line): """ Comprehensive clickbait detection extending Block 3's detect_clickbait.
New patterns added (Block 5): - Emotional manipulation - Authority false claims - Guilt/shame triggers - Overused clichés
Returns: dict: { 'is_clickbait': bool, 'severity': str, 'patterns_detected': list, 'severity_score': int, 'recommendation': str } """ patterns = [] severity_score = 0
text_lower = subject_line.lower()
# BLOCK 3 PATTERNS (Original) # 1. Excessive punctuation if re.search(r'!!!|!{2,}|\?\?\?|\?{2,}', subject_line): patterns.append('excessive_punctuation') severity_score += 2
# 2. Excessive caps all_caps_words = re.findall(r'\b[A-Z]{4,}\b', subject_line) if len(all_caps_words) >= 2: patterns.append('excessive_caps') severity_score += 2
# 3. Withheld information withheld = [ r"you won't believe", r"you'll never guess", r"this will shock", r"wait until you see", r"what happened next" ] if any(re.search(p, text_lower) for p in withheld): patterns.append('withheld_information') severity_score += 3
# 4. Exaggerated language exaggerated = ['amazing', 'shocking', 'unbelievable', 'incredible', 'insane', 'mind-blowing'] if any(word in text_lower for word in exaggerated): patterns.append('exaggerated_language') severity_score += 2
# 5. Manipulation phrases manipulation = [r"don't open unless", r"only read if", r"not for everyone"] if any(re.search(p, text_lower) for p in manipulation): patterns.append('manipulation_phrase') severity_score += 3
# BLOCK 5 PATTERNS (New) # 6. Emotional manipulation emotional = [ r"heart-wrenching", r"tear-jerking", r"soul-crushing", r"gut-wrenching", r"heartbreaking" ] if any(re.search(p, text_lower) for p in emotional): patterns.append('emotional_manipulation') severity_score += 3
# 7. Authority false claims authority = [ r"doctors hate", r"scientists discovered", r"experts don't want", r"billionaires use", r"secret.*(?:they|elite) don't" ] if any(re.search(p, text_lower) for p in authority): patterns.append('authority_false_claims') severity_score += 3
# 8. Guilt/shame triggers guilt = [ r"still doing", r"don't make this mistake again", r"why aren't you", r"everyone else knows" ] if any(re.search(p, text_lower) for p in guilt): patterns.append('guilt_shame_triggers') severity_score += 2
# 9. Overused clichés cliches = [ r"changes everything", r"game-?changer", r"the one thing", r"everything you know is wrong" ] if any(re.search(p, text_lower) for p in cliches): patterns.append('overused_cliches') severity_score += 1
# Determine severity if severity_score == 0: severity = 'none' elif severity_score <= 2: severity = 'low' elif severity_score <= 5: severity = 'medium' elif severity_score <= 8: severity = 'high' else: severity = 'critical'
is_clickbait = severity_score >= 3
result = { 'is_clickbait': is_clickbait, 'severity': severity, 'patterns_detected': patterns, 'severity_score': severity_score }
if is_clickbait: result['recommendation'] = f'{severity.upper()} clickbait detected. Rewrite subject line to be specific, honest, and value-focused.'
return result

tool.purpose: Holistic subject quality score combining multiple factors
tool.validation_logic:
import re
def score_subject_quality(subject_line): """ Holistic subject quality score.
Factors: - Length (ideal 40-60 chars) - Clickbait (severity from detect_clickbait_comprehensive) - Specificity (has specific words, numbers, details) - Formula fit (recognizable pattern)
Returns: dict: { 'quality_score': float (0-10), 'breakdown': dict, 'recommendations': list } """ score = 5.0 # Start neutral breakdown = {} recommendations = []
# Factor 1: Length length = len(subject_line) if 40 <= length <= 60: length_score = 2.5 elif 30 <= length < 40 or 60 < length <= 70: length_score = 1.5 else: length_score = 0.5
score += length_score - 1.5 # Adjust from neutral breakdown['length_score'] = length_score
if length < 30: recommendations.append('Subject too short - add more context (aim for 40-60 chars)') elif length > 60: recommendations.append('Subject too long - trim to 60 chars for mobile display')
# Factor 2: Clickbait (use comprehensive detection) clickbait_result = detect_clickbait_comprehensive(subject_line) severity_score = clickbait_result['severity_score']
if severity_score == 0: clickbait_penalty = 0 elif severity_score <= 2: clickbait_penalty = -0.5 elif severity_score <= 5: clickbait_penalty = -2.0 else: clickbait_penalty = -4.0
score += clickbait_penalty breakdown['clickbait_penalty'] = clickbait_penalty
if clickbait_result['is_clickbait']: recommendations.append(f'Clickbait detected ({clickbait_result["severity"]}) - rewrite for honesty')
# Factor 3: Specificity has_number = bool(re.search(r'\d+', subject_line)) has_specific_words = len([w for w in subject_line.split() if len(w) > 6]) >= 2
specificity_score = 0 if has_number: specificity_score += 1.0 if has_specific_words: specificity_score += 1.0
score += specificity_score - 1.0 # Adjust from neutral breakdown['specificity_score'] = specificity_score
if not has_number and not has_specific_words: recommendations.append('Add specificity - include numbers or detailed words')
# Factor 4: Formula fit (check if matches known patterns) formula_patterns = [ r'how to .* without', r'the .* (?:truth|reason|secret) about', r'\d+ (?:ways|steps|tips|mistakes)', r'what .* taught me', r'.* vs .*', r'why .* is wrong' ]
formula_fit = any(re.search(p, subject_line.lower()) for p in formula_patterns) formula_score = 1.5 if formula_fit else 0
score += formula_score - 0.75 # Adjust from neutral breakdown['formula_score'] = formula_score
if not formula_fit: recommendations.append('Consider using a proven formula for better performance')
# Cap score quality_score = max(0, min(score, 10.0))
result = { 'quality_score': round(quality_score, 1), 'breakdown': breakdown, 'recommendations': recommendations }
return result

tool.purpose: Validate subject + preview text work together, not repeat
tool.validation_logic:
def validate_preview_synergy(subject_line, preview_text): """ Validates subject + preview text synergy.
Checks: - Combined length <100 chars (mobile display) - No repetition (similar words/phrases) - Complementary info (preview adds context)
Returns: dict: { 'synergy_score': float (0-10), 'combined_length': int, 'has_repetition': bool, 'complementary': bool, 'recommendations': list } """ import re
score = 10.0 recommendations = []
# Check 1: Combined length combined_length = len(subject_line) + len(preview_text)
if combined_length <= 100: score += 0 # Ideal elif combined_length <= 120: score -= 1.0 recommendations.append('Combined length >100 chars - trim for mobile') else: score -= 2.5 recommendations.append('Combined length >120 chars - significantly trim for mobile')
# Check 2: Repetition (word overlap) subject_words = set(re.findall(r'\b\w{4,}\b', subject_line.lower())) preview_words = set(re.findall(r'\b\w{4,}\b', preview_text.lower()))
overlap = subject_words & preview_words overlap_pct = (len(overlap) / len(subject_words)) * 100 if subject_words else 0
has_repetition = overlap_pct > 40 # >40% word overlap = repetition
if has_repetition: score -= 3.0 recommendations.append(f'High word overlap ({overlap_pct:.0f}%) - preview should complement, not repeat')
# Check 3: Complementary (preview adds info not in subject) unique_preview_words = preview_words - subject_words complementary = len(unique_preview_words) >= 2
if complementary: score += 0 # Good else: score -= 1.5 recommendations.append('Preview doesn\'t add new info - make it complementary')
synergy_score = max(0, min(score, 10.0))
result = { 'synergy_score': round(synergy_score, 1), 'combined_length': combined_length, 'has_repetition': has_repetition, 'complementary': complementary, 'overlap_percentage': round(overlap_pct, 1), 'recommendations': recommendations }
return result
